message:
feat(storage,share): notification signal clearing, repo-filter tree helpers, orphan project scope detection

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs

- New `clear_notification_signal(project_slug)` tears down any
  pending notification-signal marker for a project so a subsequent
  write does not pick up stale state. The prior reset path just
  flipped an in-memory flag; the persistent marker file lived on
  past it, and a second writer opening the same project would see
  the old marker and re-deliver a stale notification. Remove the
  marker file (tolerating ENOENT) and clear the in-memory flag in
  one call so the two states can never drift.
- `repo_path_matches_filter(candidate, filter)` and
  `tree_contains_prefix(tree, prefix)` are new private helpers used
  by the repository scan path to decide whether a git tree entry
  matches a caller-supplied filter. Replace a hand-rolled
  string-compare loop that did not handle component boundaries
  correctly (it would happily match `frontend` as a prefix of
  `frontend-experiments`). The new `tree_contains_prefix` walks the
  git2 tree component-by-component and returns true only when the
  full prefix is present as a sequence of tree entries.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/scope.rs

- `load_scope_projects(conn)` returns the full set of
  `ProjectRecord`s that the share-scope logic needs to consider,
  pulled from one consistent read so later rows cannot disagree
  with earlier rows because of an interleaved write.
- `collect_orphan_project_ids(...)` identifies project IDs that
  appear in the scope's linkage tables but have no corresponding
  project row in the projects table. These are leftovers from a
  previous partial delete — deleting the row but not the linkage —
  and the share scope used to silently include them in the scope,
  producing bundles that referenced dangling ids.
- `collect_orphan_project_ids_from_query(...)` is the generic form
  that takes a pre-computed project id set and returns the orphan
  subset, so the same logic can be reused by the finalize path.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/finalize.rs

- finalize now calls `collect_orphan_project_ids_from_query` before
  writing the share manifest and refuses to finalize a scope that
  references orphan project ids. The caller has to either re-run
  the scope against the current project set or explicitly prune
  the orphans. This closes a loophole where a share bundle could
  be committed that pointed at project ids no reader could resolve.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/snapshot.rs

Snapshot now honors the scope's orphan-filtered project id set so
snapshots taken after a partial delete do not include dangling ids.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/bundle.rs

Single-line import fix so the bundle path can call the new orphan
helpers.

files.patch:
@@ -1529,6 +1529,7 @@ mod tests {
             messages: 0,
             recipients: 0,
             file_reservations: 0,
+            inbox_stats: 0,
             agent_links: 0,
             project_sibling_suggestions: 0,
         }
